title: How To Convince People 2 Like Appreciate Evangelion
display_title: How To Convince People 2 Like Appreciate Evangelion
wikitext:
[]: How To Convince People 2 Like/Appreciate Evangelion Post by juaxoo » Thu Mar 31, 2011 4:39 am as topic, how do i make eva appeal more to ppl i noe, who doesn't seem to like it?, or worst of all, who hates it for no good reason and makes fun of other ppl for liking it. Got any tips, fellow EGF memebers? *wink wink*

[]: Post by Xard » Thu Mar 31, 2011 4:42 am Don't try. If they like it they like it, if they don't like it then they don't like it.

[]: Post by symbv » Thu Mar 31, 2011 5:02 am I second Xard. You will only be hurt like Shinji if you try. The thing is: If the person is neutral about the anime, it may still possible, but if the guy hates it or make fun of it, then I do not see how he can be made to change his mind. He would just harden his heart and make even crueller jokes to resist any attempt of persuasion.

[]: Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Mar 31, 2011 5:03 am Show them the manga or something. If they don´t like it, they don´t.

[]: Post by Combs » Thu Mar 31, 2011 5:13 am I've only recommended it to two friends. We saw it over the course of a few weeks, and in the end he probably enjoyed it. He got me to watch GiTS as well. I've introduced it to another friend today, in fact, and he seems to be enjoying it. So if you absolutely must, choose your introverted friends, boring hipsters, or weeaboo faggots :3c

[]: Post by juaxoo » Thu Mar 31, 2011 10:33 am Xard wrote: Don't try. If they like it they like it, if they don't like it then they don't like it.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 31, 2011 10:36 am You can't MAKE someone like something. If you try to force them to, they'll hate it even more. This is a pointless discussion.

[]: Post by juaxoo » Thu Mar 31, 2011 10:37 am The Eva Monkey wrote: You can't MAKE someone like something. If you try to force them to, they'll hate it even more. This is a pointless discussion.

[]: Post by Mordicaii » Thu Mar 31, 2011 10:44 am juaxoo wrote: im not trying to make/force them into liking it.., im searchign for ways that might convince them, start a spark in them, incept an idea in them.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 31, 2011 10:49 am juaxoo wrote: im not trying to make/force them into liking it.., im searchign for ways that might convince them, start a spark in them, incept an idea in them. am i not making myself clear enough?

[]: Post by juaxoo » Thu Mar 31, 2011 10:58 am The Eva Monkey wrote: You can't CONVINCE someone to like something. If you try to force them to, they'll hate it even more. This is a pointless discussion. Trust me. I have a lot of friends who are into anime. I would love for more of them to share in my love of Eva, but the last thing any of them want is to have it forced on them. They have to approach it on their own. Once that foundation exists, you can build upon it. But until they have that foundation, you will only drive them further away by trying to influence them.

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 31, 2011 11:08 am juaxoo wrote: ok i guess that makes sense, thanks sorry i was looking forward to making more ppl like eva and make them see the goodness in it too much and lost my rationality

[]: Post by Sharaz Destler » Thu Mar 31, 2011 11:44 am All I would say is, "Give it a few episodes."

[]: Post by InstrumentalityOne » Thu Mar 31, 2011 11:45 am OP reminds me of people who buy "Manipulating People for Dummies"(or something in the vein of that), sort of.

[]: Post by juaxoo » Thu Mar 31, 2011 11:47 am InstrumentalityOne wrote: OP reminds me of people who buy "Manipulating People for Dummies"(or something in the vein of that), sort of.

[]: Post by Xard » Thu Mar 31, 2011 11:50 am It's a shame you can't write text that doesn't make my head hurt

[]: Post by juaxoo » Thu Mar 31, 2011 11:58 am Xard wrote: It's a shame you can't write text that doesn't make my head hurt

[]: Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Mar 31, 2011 12:10 pm Xard wrote: It's a shame you can't write text that doesn't make my head hurt
